Object.Name: Zigbee Device
Object.Description1: Individual Zigbee device representation. Device info, status monitoring, ZCL attribute access, and device management.

Item.Name: IEEE Address
Item.Description: 64-bit IEEE MAC address in hexadecimal format (16 characters). Globally unique device identifier burned into hardware. Used for all device communications and binding operations. Never changes during device lifetime.

Item.Name: Network Address
Item.Description: 16-bit network address (0x0000-0xFFFF) assigned by coordinator during joining. Used for routing within network. May change if device rejoins or network performs address reassignment. 0xFFFF indicates unassigned.

Item.Name: Device Type
Item.Description: Zigbee device type: 0=Unknown, 1=Coordinator, 2=Router (mains-powered, extends network), 3=End Device (battery or mains, doesn't route), 4=Sleepy End Device (battery-powered, low power mode). Determines routing and power management behavior.

Item.Name: Manufacturer
Item.Description: Manufacturer name read from device Basic cluster (ZCL attribute 0x0004). May be empty if device doesn't support this attribute or hasn't been interviewed. Used for device identification and compatibility checking.

Item.Name: Model
Item.Description: Model identifier read from device Basic cluster (ZCL attribute 0x0005). Specific product model name or number. Used for device-specific features and troubleshooting. May be empty if not supported.

Item.Name: Firmware Version
Item.Description: Device firmware version read from Basic cluster. Used for OTA upgrade management and compatibility checking. Format varies by manufacturer. Monitor for available updates.

Item.Name: Power Source
Item.Description: Device power source: 0=Unknown, 1=Mains (AC powered, always on), 2=Battery (portable, may sleep), 3=DC (external DC supply). Affects polling strategy and sleep behavior. Battery devices require poll control for timely updates.

Item.Name: LQI
Item.Description: Link Quality Indicator (0-255). Measures communication quality between device and coordinator/parent. Higher is better. <50=poor, 50-150=fair, 150-200=good, >200=excellent. Monitor for connectivity issues.

Item.Name: RSSI
Item.Description: Received Signal Strength Indicator in dBm. Negative values, closer to 0 is stronger. >-70=excellent, -70 to -80=good, -80 to -90=fair, <-90=poor. Use for network planning and troubleshooting.

Item.Name: Last Seen
Item.Description: Timestamp of last communication from device (Unix epoch). Updated on any message reception. Used to detect offline devices. Devices not seen for >5 minutes may be offline. Battery devices may report infrequently.

Item.Name: Endpoints
Item.Description: Active endpoint numbers (1-254). Each endpoint represents a logical device function (e.g., endpoint 1=main light, endpoint 2=button). Discovered during interview. Multiple endpoints enable complex multi-function devices.

Item.Name: Clusters
Item.Description: JSON array of supported ZCL clusters by endpoint. Format: [{"endpoint":1,"in":[0,3,4,5,6],"out":[]}]. "in" clusters are server (device provides), "out" are client (device controls). Determines device capabilities.

Item.Name: Interviewed
Item.Description: Interview completion status. True when device capabilities have been fully discovered (endpoints, clusters, attributes). False for newly joined devices awaiting interview. Trigger interview manually if needed.

Item.Name: Online
Item.Description: Current online status. True if device responded recently (within last 5 minutes). False if offline/unreachable. Battery devices may appear offline between poll intervals. Use Ping command to verify.

Item.Name: Profile ID
Item.Description: Zigbee profile ID. Common values: 0x0104=Home Automation, 0xC05E=Light Link, 0x0109=Smart Energy, 0xA1E0=Green Power. Determines which clusters and features are available. Most devices use Home Automation profile.

Item.Name: Device ID
Item.Description: Zigbee device identifier within profile. Examples: 0x0100=On/Off Light, 0x0101=Dimmable Light, 0x010D=Extended Color Light, 0x0302=Temperature Sensor, 0x0402=IAS Zone. Indicates device function and required clusters.

Item.Name: Remove
Item.Description: Execute to remove device from network. Sends leave request to device and removes from coordinator database. Device must be factory reset to rejoin. Use for decommissioning devices or clearing failed joins.

Item.Name: Interview
Item.Description: Execute to start device interview process. Queries endpoints, clusters, and basic attributes. Takes 5-30 seconds. Automatically performed on join but can be triggered manually if initial interview fails or device capabilities change.

Item.Name: Ping
Item.Description: Execute to ping device and verify connectivity. Updates Last Seen timestamp and Online status. Useful for checking battery devices or troubleshooting connectivity issues. Returns success if device responds.

Item.Name: Read Attribute
Item.Description: Execute to read ZCL attribute from device. Argument format: [endpoint][clusterId][attributeId] (5 bytes). Example: Read OnOff state: [01][0006][0000]. Returns attribute value. Used for querying device state.

Item.Name: Write Attribute
Item.Description: Execute to write ZCL attribute to device. Argument format: [endpoint][clusterId][attributeId][dataType][value]. Example: Set level to 128: [01][0008][0000][20][80]. Used for device configuration and control.
